content:
2 min read
Just now

--

If you started a frontend project in 2020 and another one in 2026, the tooling would look completely different.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Webpack lost its dominance.
Vite became the default.
Rspack proved Rust could replace years of JavaScript infrastructure.
Turbopack pushed incremental execution even further.
Developers won.
Builds became dramatically faster.
Yet something strange happened.
Organizations kept paying to recompute the same work every day.
The Problem Nobody Talks About
Imagine a company with 200 engineers.
Every morning…
200 laptops build.
Every pull request builds.
Every CI runner builds.
Every deployment builds.
Most of that computation is identical.
Yet every stage proves the same facts independently.
Why?
We’ve Been Optimizing Execution
For years we optimized execution.
Better bundlers.
Better compilers.
Better incremental builds.
Better output caches.
But all of them start from the same assumption.
Every execution must establish its own truth.
What If That Assumption Is Wrong?
What if development, CI and production didn’t have to rediscover the same build knowledge independently?
What if valid work became portable?
Not cached.
Portable.
What if software delivery had one source of truth?
Welcome to the Build Authority Era
I believe the next generation of developer tooling won’t compete on execution speed alone.
It will compete on build authority.
One verified source of truth consumed by every stage of software delivery.
That’s the idea we’re building with Ionify.
Ending
The build wars aren’t ending.
They’re moving one layer deeper.
The next question isn’t:
“How can builds become faster?”
It’s:
“Why should identical work be proven again at all?”
